name: Eclipse JDT Language Server
description: Java language server providing code completion, go-to-definition, refactoring, diagnostics, and project-aware intelligence for Maven and Gradle projects
when_to_use: Always active for Java projects — provides IDE intelligence including type inference, imports, and refactoring

name: mvn compile
description: Compile a Java Maven project and resolve all dependencies from pom.xml
when_to_use: To compile the project, resolve dependencies, or check for compilation errors in Maven-based projects

install.command: brew install maven || sdk install maven

name: gradle build
description: Compile and assemble a Java Gradle project including all dependencies from build.gradle
when_to_use: To compile, assemble, and package Gradle-based Java projects

install.command: brew install gradle || sdk install gradle

name: mvn test
description: Run unit tests in a Maven project using Surefire plugin — executes JUnit and TestNG tests
when_to_use: After code changes, before commits, in CI pipelines for Maven projects
what_it_finds: Test failures, assertion errors, uncaught exceptions, coverage gaps

install.command: brew install maven || sdk install maven

name: gradle test
description: Run unit tests in a Gradle project — executes JUnit and TestNG tests with parallel support
when_to_use: After code changes, before commits, in CI pipelines for Gradle projects
what_it_finds: Test failures, assertion errors, uncaught exceptions

install.command: brew install gradle || sdk install gradle

name: JUnit Console Runner
description: Standalone JUnit 5 test runner — execute tests directly without Maven or Gradle
when_to_use: Run individual test classes or methods directly, useful for quick iteration and debugging tests
what_it_finds: Test failures, assertion errors, test discovery issues

name: Checkstyle
description: Java static analysis tool enforcing coding standards — checks naming conventions, Javadoc, whitespace, imports, and design patterns
when_to_use: Before commits, in CI — enforce consistent code style and catch style violations
what_it_finds: Naming violations, missing Javadoc, import order issues, line length violations, coding standard breaches

install.command: brew install checkstyle || download from https://checkstyle.org

name: SpotBugs
description: Static analysis tool that finds bug patterns in Java bytecode — detects null pointer dereferences, infinite loops, resource leaks, and concurrency issues
when_to_use: After compilation, in CI — catches real bugs that compile but are likely wrong
what_it_finds: Null pointer dereferences, resource leaks, thread safety issues, performance anti-patterns, dead code

install.command: brew install spotbugs || download from https://spotbugs.github.io

name: PMD
description: Source code analyzer finding common programming flaws — unused variables, empty catch blocks, unnecessary object creation, and copy-paste detection
when_to_use: Before commits, in CI — finds code smells and potential bugs at source level
what_it_finds: Unused variables, empty catch blocks, dead code, copy-paste issues (CPD), overcomplicated expressions

install.command: brew install pmd || download from https://pmd.github.io

name: OWASP Dependency-Check
description: Scan Java project dependencies for known CVEs using the National Vulnerability Database — identifies vulnerable libraries in Maven and Gradle projects
when_to_use: Before releases, in CI, after dependency updates — critical for security compliance
what_it_finds: CVEs in dependencies, outdated vulnerable libraries, transitive dependency risks

name: Spotless
description: Code formatter for Java supporting google-java-format, eclipse, and palantir styles — integrates with Maven and Gradle
when_to_use: Before every commit to ensure consistent code formatting across the project

install.command: Add com.diffplug.spotless plugin to build.gradle or pom.xml

name: mvn release
description: Automate Maven project releases — bump version, create git tag, deploy artifacts to repository
when_to_use: When publishing releases, cutting new versions of Maven artifacts

install.command: brew install maven || sdk install maven

name: Gradle Wrapper
description: Generate or update the Gradle wrapper — ensures all developers and CI use the same Gradle version without a local install
when_to_use: When setting up a new Gradle project or upgrading the Gradle version

install.command: brew install gradle || sdk install gradle

name: jdb
description: Java command-line debugger — attach to running JVM processes, set breakpoints, inspect variables, and step through code
when_to_use: Debugging Java applications, inspecting runtime state, diagnosing crashes or unexpected behavior

name: Java Flight Recorder
description: Low-overhead JVM profiler built into the JDK — records CPU usage, memory allocation, garbage collection, thread contention, and I/O events
when_to_use: Performance profiling, diagnosing slow methods, memory leaks, GC pressure, or thread contention
what_it_finds: Hot methods, memory allocation sites, GC pauses, lock contention, I/O bottlenecks

name: async-profiler
description: Low-overhead sampling profiler for JVM — generates CPU and allocation flame graphs without safepoint bias
when_to_use: Performance analysis requiring accurate flame graphs, profiling CPU hotspots or memory allocation patterns
what_it_finds: CPU hotspots, allocation-heavy code paths, lock contention, native method overhead
